hypothesis: Design evidence compiled into memory and slide primitives should improve generated PPT output compared with a prompt-only baseline, while deliberately weak bad-data rules should degrade semantic quality without breaking structural delivery.

arms.input_boundary: baseline_prompt.md only; design_memory.json, tutorial_notes.md, vulca_generation_brief.md, and vulca_ppt_skill.md excluded while writing slide modules.
arms.expected_signal: Baseline comparator should be structurally valid and comparatively generic because it only receives the baseline prompt.
arms.observed_signal: Valid editable deck with named experiment arms and gates, but Gemini characterized it as more technical specification/testing dashboard than polished product surface.
generation_warnings: artifact-tool layout QA reported 0 errors and 24 warnings, mostly tight text or table split-inline warnings
Gemini review is qualitative evidence only

arms.input_boundary: vulca_generation_brief.md, design_memory.json, deck_outline.json, tutorial-derived rules, and product-lab slide primitives.
arms.expected_signal: Full Vulca should show stronger product-lab surfaces: Brief Cockpit, Learning Map, Experiment Lab, QA Gate, Product Decision.
arms.observed_signal: Strongest arm: specific product-lab run report with schemas, boundaries, pipeline stages, memory compiler, code-generation anatomy, QA gate, and repaired score-delta/ablation slides.
generation_warnings: artifact-tool layout QA reported 0 errors and 25 warnings after repair, mostly tight text or table split-inline warnings
Gemini review is qualitative evidence only
repair_notes: Slide 10 decision table updated with reviewed-public-blocked decision.

arms.input_boundary: bad_data_generation_brief.md with deliberately weak but structurally valid rules.
arms.expected_signal: Bad-data should remain structurally valid while visibly weaker through generic card grids, mismatched hierarchy, weak source boundaries, and poor product-surface feel.
arms.observed_signal: Structurally valid but semantically degraded through generic workflows, poor claim-to-proof relationship, non-committal content, and weaker source-boundary discipline.
generation_warnings: artifact-tool layout QA reported 0 errors and 26 warnings, mostly tight text or table split-inline warnings
degraded semantic quality supports negative-control signal but does not prove automatic corruption detection

remaining_uncertainty: Native PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides render fidelity has not been executed.
Human review has not approved public publishing.
Gemini review is qualitative evidence, not final approval.
The negative control demonstrates visible degradation but not automatic runtime rejection.
